Summary: MyoD1-centered, causal chain. Role(context): MyoD1 is a master myogenic transcription factor in mouse C2C12 myoblasts and mouse heart that drives myogenic differentiation and enforces cell-cycle exit. Mechanism: In TAFAZZIN‑knockout (TAZ‑KO) C2C12 myoblasts and TAZ‑KO mouse hearts, loss of TAFAZZIN is associated with decreased Wnt secretion/signaling (reduced Wls, Porcn, Axin2), which correlates with upregulation of the MyoD1 transcriptional repressor MKX; elevated MKX transcriptionally represses MyoD1. Outcome: Repressed MyoD1 in TAZ‑KO cells coincides with severe inhibition of myogenesis (absence of MHC), and restoring MyoD1 (or activating Wnt) rescues MyoD1 expression and restores MHC/fusion in differentiation assays.
References: Vo, L., Schmidtke, M. W., Da Rosa-Junior, N. T., Ren, M., Schlame, M., & Greenberg, M. L. (2023). Cardiolipin metabolism regulates expression of muscle transcription factor MyoD1 and muscle development. J Biol Chem, 299(3), 102978. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jbc.2023.102978
